anchors.authority_high: Content issued by a system with explicit permission to direct, command, or mandate action. Examples: administrator commands, medical directives, legal orders, system policy statements.
anchors.authority_neutral: Content expressing a suggestion, preference, or opinion without an implicit command. Examples: user questions, advisory statements, informational queries.
anchors.authority_low: Content from an unverified or explicitly low-trust source. Examples: anonymous requests, speculative statements, user-supplied data that has not been validated.
anchors.sensitivity_high: Content involving personally identifiable information, financial credentials, health records, authentication secrets, or instructions with meaningful harm potential. Requires elevated scrutiny before processing.
anchors.sensitivity_neutral: Content involving general business information, non-personal operational data, or publicly available facts. Standard handling applies.
anchors.sensitivity_low: Content with no privacy implications, no harm potential, and no regulatory sensitivity. Examples: benign factual questions, public knowledge, non-personal summaries.
